title: Narcissus quintanilhae
taxonomy: Narcissus quintanilhae is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the genus Narcissus, which comprises several species of daffodils and jonquils. The plant is native to Portugal and is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and overcollection. Its classification also places it in the order Asparagales, which includes various families of flowering plants. Further classification includes its placement in the family Amaryllidaceae and the tribe Leucojoidae.

intro: Narcissus quintanilhae, a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, is a bulbous perennial native to Portugal. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory infections and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Narcissus quintanilhae include alkaloids, such as galantamine and lycorine, which have been found to possess neuroprotective and acetylcholinesterase-inhibiting activities. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Portuguese medicine to treat a range of conditions, including coughs and colds.
